content: n8n's credential system is decoupled from canvas nodes: credentials are named, stored centrally, and referenced by nodes rather than pasted inline. On expiry/revocation, n8n does NOT proactively invalidate the node visually ahead of time — the canvas shows a generic red 'failed' badge on the node only when a run actually hits the auth error (e.g. 'invalid_grant: token expired or revoked'), and clicking the failed node surfaces the raw provider error text. Recovery UX is via a 'Reconnect Account'/'Reauthorize' action on the credential from the node's credential dropdown or the central Credentials panel — not a proactive expiry warning shown in advance.
